rationale: The page contains detailed information on three distinct health-related topics: Transfats (definition, health impacts, and regulatory initiatives), Weight Loss Drugs (Wegovy, semaglutide, and the GLP-1 mechanism), and Oral Rehydration Salts (FSSAI directives, WHO-recommended composition, and physiological functions). This provides ample material for multiple-choice questions regarding public health and pharmacology.
cleanedText:
• Anchor Institution: National Institute of One Health, Nagpur.
• Need of the Mission: Zoonotic Risk Mitigation, Pandemic Preparedness, curbing AMR, etc.
5.6.6. TRANSFATS
The WHO officially recognized four countries (Austria, Norway, Oman and Singapore) for their outstanding leadership in eliminating industrially produced trans fats.
About Trans fats (Trans-fatty acids (TFA))
• Definition: TFA are unsaturated fatty acids that can be industrially produced (via hydrogenation) or naturally found in animal-source foods (meat, milk).
o Industrially produced trans fats are commonly found in baked goods like biscuits, margarine, and Vanaspati ghee, etc.
• Health Impacts:
o Raises the level of the bad cholesterol and low density lipoprotein-cholesterol (LDL-c).
o They have been also linked to inflammation, overweight/obesity, high blood pressure, diabetes and some types of cancers.
Key Initiative taken
• WHO’s "REPLACE" Action Package
• India enforced limiting trans fats in oils to no more than 2 % through the Food Safety and Standards (Prohibition and Restrictions on Sales) Second Amendment Regulations, 2021.
Other Types of Fat
• UNSATURATED FATS (Good Fats)
o Found in nuts, avocados and other vegetables.
o Lower in calories than other fats.
• SATURATED FATS
o Found mostly in animal products.
o Excessive consumption is unhealthy.
5.6.7. WEIGHT LOSS DRUG
Danish pharma company Novo Nordisk launched its weight loss drug Wegovy in India.
• The drug semaglutide, marketed under the brand name Wegovy, will be available in the form of injections.
o Semaglutide, works as an appetite suppressant by imitating a hormone called glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1).
o GLP-1 is an intestinal hormone released after eating, and usually makes people feel fuller.
• Other weight loss drugs include Ozempic, Mounjaro (made up of tirzepatide (mimics function of GLP-1 and glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP)), etc.
5.6.8. ORAL REHYDRATION SALTS (ORS)
Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) has directed states and UTs to remove beverages and similar products being marketed using the term ORS.
About ORS
• ORS term is associated exclusively with WHO-recommended Oral Rehydration Salts, which are drug products.
• Purpose: It is a simple, low-cost glucose electrolyte solution used to prevent and treat dehydration from acute diarrhoeal diseases.
• WHO recommended ORS salts composition: Sodium chloride, glucose (anhydrous), potassium chloride, and trisodium citrate (dihydrate).
• Functions: Glucose enhances sodium and water absorption, sodium and potassium replace essential electrolyte losses, and citrate helps correct dehydration-related acidosis.
• Developed by Dilip Mahalanabis.
